objective: Add rafka-broker binary. Same shape as gateway. Two services visible in Jaeger. Wire Principle #10 span attributes on all substrate spans. Verify iroh 0.91 removes dedicated-thread workaround.
in_scope: broker/ binary — clone the gateway boot-span chain pattern; service_name = rafkav2-broker; node_type = broker
Both binaries share crates/rafka-telemetry init code
Principle #9 carryover: iroh bumped from 0.35 -> 0.91 (latest stable non-RC; 1.0.0-rc.0 exists but is pre-release)
Principle #9 carryover: dedicated-thread workaround DELETED — iroh 0.91 binds in <200ms on multi-thread tokio runtime on Windows; COM deadlock is fixed upstream

Principle #10 carryover: all substrate spans now carry required attributes (node_id, node_type, bind_addr, version, alpn, peer_count, reason) via info_span! macro with tracing::field::Empty + Span::current().record() for late-binding
out_of_scope: Peer discovery between gateway + broker — Sprint 03
exit_criteria: cargo run -p rafka-broker boots, idles emitting heartbeats — DONE
Jaeger shows TWO services (rafkav2-gateway + rafkav2-broker) each with their own boot-span chain — DONE
Both binaries running concurrently — no port conflicts (iroh binds 0.0.0.0:0 ephemeral) — DONE
All substrate spans carry locked attributes per Principle #10 — DONE
iroh 0.91 direct bind tested: PASSED in <200ms on Windows multi-thread runtime — DONE
